methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $13M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Transportation (found 1 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 5 same-sector peers exist in Transportation, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

private.name: REGENT Craft
private.rationale: Same sector (Transportation), same stage (Series A), post-money $270M

data.narrative:
Sarla Aviation is a Series A transportation company founded by Rakesh Gaonkar and Adrian Schmidt, having raised $13M to date. The transportation sector comp universe consists of 8 private peers, including REGENT Craft, Rivian, The Boring Company, Einride, and Harbinger; no public comparables exist in a sufficiently narrow peer set.

Valuation triangulation relied on a single applicable methodology due to constraints in the peer dataset. The Stage-Progression method applies a 2.0x step-up multiplier from Series A to the next financing stage, using the $13M total raised as a proxy for current valuation. This yields an implied valuation of $26M. The Peer-Median approach was not applicable because only one same-stage transportation comparable existed; neither adjacent-stage nor similar-tier peers provided the minimum three datapoints required for meaningful median analysis. Drawing on an all-sector median would introduce unacceptable noise given the specialized nature of frontier transportation.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $22M (low) to $30M (high), with a median point of $26M. This range reflects single-method triangulation and carries material uncertainty. Key limitations include the sparse comparable set within transportation at Series A stage and reliance on the raised-capital proxy rather than market-derived multiples. Further refinement would require either additional documented transactions in the frontier transportation subsector or willingness to broaden peer parameters at the cost of comparability precision.
